back_translation: There was a man among the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a member of the council of the Jews.
This man came to Jesus at night, and said to him, "Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one is able to perform these signs that you do unless God is dwelling with him."
Jesus answered and said to him, "Truly, truly I tell you, if anyone is not born again, that one cannot see the kingdom of God."
Nicodemus said to him, "How can a person be born again when he is already old? Can he go back into his mother's womb a second time and be born again?"
Jesus answered, "Truly, truly I tell you, if anyone is not born of water and the Spirit, that one cannot enter the kingdom of God.
What is born of flesh is flesh, and what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
Do not be amazed that I have told you, 'You all must be born again.'
The wind blows where its will desires, you hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes; so it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit."
Nicodemus answered him, "How can these things be possible?"
Jesus answered him, "You are a teacher of Israel, and you do not understand these things?
Truly, truly I tell you, we speak what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, but you all do not accept our testimony.
If I have told you of earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you of heavenly things?
No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, namely the Son of Man.
Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
so that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.
For God loved the world to such a degree that he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in that Son will not perish, but have eternal life.
For God did not send his Son into the world in order to judge the world, but in order that the world might receive salvation through that Son.
The one who believes in the Son is not judged, but the one who does not believe is already judged, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.
And the verdict is this: the light has come into the world, but people loved the darkness more than the light, because their deeds were evil.
For everyone who practices evil hates the light and does not come to the light, lest his deeds be exposed.
But the one who lives according to the truth comes to the light, so that it may be clearly shown that his deeds have been done in God."
After these things, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, and he was staying there with them and was baptizing.
And John was also baptizing at the village of Aenon near the town of Salim, because there was much water there, and people came in groups to receive baptism.
For John had not yet been arrested into prison.
At that time a dispute arose between John's disciples and a certain Jew about the purification according to custom.

John's disciples came to him and said to him, "Rabbi, the one who was with you on the eastern side of the Jordan River, the one whom you testified about — behold, that one is now baptizing, and everyone is going to him."
John answered, "A person can receive nothing unless it has been given to him from heaven.
You yourselves are witnesses for me that I said, 'I am not the Christ, but I have been sent before him.'

The one who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom who stands and waits to listen to his voice rejoices greatly because of the voice of the bridegroom. So this joy of mine is now full.
He must increase, but I must decrease.
The one who comes from above is above all things; the one who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one who comes from the earth; the one who comes from heaven is above all things.
He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
Whoever accepts his testimony, that one seals/certifies that God is true.
For the one whom God sent speaks the words of God, for God does not give the Spirit by limit/measure.
The Father loves the Son, and has placed all things in the hand of the Son.
The one who believes in the Son has eternal life, but the one who refuses to obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him."
